channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-04
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. The `scienceverse/metacheck` project has made notable advancements with the introduction of transparent resource caps, LLM/download controls, and archive tooling on the `data_check` branch. Meanwhile, `MicheleNuijten/statcheck` and `giladfeldman/escicheck` have focused on integrating changes and synchronizing with other repositories, highlighting the connections between our projects. Additionally, `giladfeldman/docpluck` and `Davidvandijcke/coarse` have made fixes and updates to their respective main and dev branches, demonstrating the community's commitment to continuous improvement. The `giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward` project has also made its presence known on the Chrome Webstore, and `shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve` has revised its validation note and added a citation, showcasing the diversity of our community's work. These updates reflect the collaborative spirit and dedication to open-source research that our community embodies.

64 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: statcheck

Most active:

docpluck31 commits
regcheck18 commits
escicheck6 commits
metacheck5 commits
statcheck2 commits

item.title: metacheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

Work was done on the `data_check` branch, where the first version of datacheck modules was added. The next day, additions were made to the `data_check` branch, although the specifics of these additions are not detailed. On July 4, 2026, transparent resource caps, LLM/download controls, data-manifest, and archive tooling were added to the `data_check` branch, as stated in the commit message. The `data_check` branch saw the most significant changes with the introduction of these features. The commits suggest a progression from initial module creation to adding more substantial features like resource caps and tooling. Overall, the `data_check` branch was the sole focus of these commits.

2 commit(s) today:

  • c42ecbf [data_check] Transparent resource caps, LLM/download controls, data-manifest + archive tooling — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: statcheck: activity update
item.description:
MicheleNuijten/statcheck

The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was worked on, where a merge was performed from the same branch of the `https://github.com/MicheleNuijten/statcheck` repository. The merge commit on the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was made to integrate changes from the remote repository. Additionally, on the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch, "admin updates" were made, as per the commit message. No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The changes were limited to the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch, with no other branches or modules mentioned.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 14 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where a commit addressed the issue of "chan_feldman T3 caption-tail-walk TEXT-LOSS" by adding a diagnostic and an xfail guard, with the fix being deferred as it requires corpus-wide changes. Another commit on the 'main' branch updated the documentation to record the outcome of "run-3" and resolved "W0l ×-as-3 residuals", also rescoping "efendic headings to B6" as part of the documentation triage. A fix was applied to the 'glyph' module on the 'main' branch to recover two residual "×-as-3 prose shapes" that "W0k can't reach", related to "efendic" and version "v2.4.116". Additionally, a rendering fix was made on the 'main' branch to remove an orphaned affiliation remnant in the Abstract body, related to "chandrashekar" and version "v2.4.115".

14 commit(s) today:

  • ba4e124 [main] docs+test(rc-t): diagnose chan_feldman T3 caption-tail-walk TEXT-LOSS; xfail guard (fix is corpus-wide, deferred) — Gilad Feldman
  • 56f60b5 [main] docs(triage): record run-3 outcome — W0l ×-as-3 residuals resolved (v2.4.116); efendic headings rescoped to B6 — Gilad Feldman
  • a4f8796 [main] fix(glyph): recover 2 residual ×-as-3 prose shapes W0k can't reach (efendic) + v2.4.116 — Gilad Feldman
  • e140cf1 [main] fix(render): strip affiliation remnant orphaned in Abstract body (chandrashekar) + v2.4.115 — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
